english: as to (stature, rank etc.) mtó-na (mtho na) when I am high, when I rise; mtó-ba ɣnón-pa (mtho ba gnon pa) to lower what is high, to bring down, to humble, frq.; ṅas mto-mtó byás-pas dma-dmá byuṅ (ngas mtho mtho byas pas dma' dma' byung) the more I was aspiring, the more I was brought low Pth.; sbyin-pa mtó-ba (sbyin pa mtho ba) Stg. was explained: gifts or alms bestowed from a sincere heart. — 2. hammer, v. to-ba (tho ba); mto-po-tog (mtho po thog) a stone used as a hammer Cs. Comp.: mto-ḱyad (mtho khyad) height, highness Dzl — mto-dógs (mtho dogs) Pth. (together with ɣyo-sgyú (g.yo sgyu), and prag-dóg (phrag dog)) perh. mistrust, suspicion; *tóṅ-dod-čaṅ* (*thong dod can*) W. ambitious, aspiring, aiming at things too high. — mto-spyód (mtho spyod) W. a haughty manner. — mto-dmán (mtho dman) 1. Cs. high and low, uneven; also Schr. 2. height, mto-dmán mnyám-pa (mtho dman mnyam pa) of equal height Glr. — mto-tsám(s)-pa (mtho tsam(s) pa) v. to-tsám-pa (tho tsam pa). — mto-rís (mtho ris) heaven, abode of the gods, paradise, Elysium.
Sch., mtóṅs-ka (mthongs ka) Pth., chest, breast, mtóṅ-ga-nas _dzín-pa (mthong ga nas 'dzin pa) to seize by the breast Pth.

to see, 1. vb. n. to have the power of vision, often with mig(-yis) (mig(-yis)); mtóṅ-bar _gyúr-ba (mthong bar 'gyur ba) to obtain the faculty of seeing, to recover one's sight; mtóṅ-bar byéd-pa (mthong bar byed pa) to make (the blind) see Dzl.; mig-gis nye mtoṅ riṅ mi mtoṅ (mig gis nye mthong ring mi mthong) he sees only when the object is near, not when it is far, he is short-sighted Med.; nye-mtóṅ (nye mthong) short-sighted Sch. — 2. vb. a. 1. to perceive, by the eye, to see, to behold, bód-kyi ri mtoṅ-bai ri (bod kyi ri mthong ba'i ri) an eminence from whence one can see the mountains of Tibet Glr.; mi ɣzán-gyis mtóṅ-sar (mi gzhan gyis mthong sar) (a place) where one can be seen by others; de bú-mos mtóṅ-bar mdzád-do (de bu mos mthong bar mdzad do) he made her see it Dzl.; mtóṅ-ba źig yód-na (mthong ba zhig yod na) if there is one that has seen it, if there exists a witness Dzl.; de mtóṅ-ste śes (de mthong ste shes) seeing this, I came to know, i.e. from this I saw, I perceived; mtoṅ tos dran reg (mthong thos dran reg), frq., the seeing, hearing, touching, thinking of (e.g. a form of prayer, or magic formula); ma _óṅ-bar (ma 'ong bar), (or _óṅ-ba ('ong ba)) mtóṅ-nas (mthong nas) as he saw his mother coming. 2. with accus. and termin.: to regard, consider, take for, Thgy.; rdzas dkar sér-por mtoṅ (rdzas dkar ser por mthong) Lt. taking white things for yellow ones. 3. to meet, find, catch. 4. to know, understand, perceive (mentally) Mil. 5. col. to undergo, suffer, endure, misfortunes, pain etc. (cf. stonpa (ston pa) 4), mi mtoṅ mtóṅ-ba (mi mthong mthong ba) to suffer what is not to be suffered, not bearable nif., cf. ltá-ba (lta ba). Comp. mtóṅ-kuṅ (mthong khung) Cs. 'a window', prob. for mtóṅs-kuṅ (mthongs khung). — mtóṅ-sgom-čan (mthong sgom can) Thgy. was explained: one who instantly knows and understands every thing he sees (?) — mtóṅ-ḱor (mthong khor), mtóṅ-mta (mthong mtha'), the reach of sight, range of vision Cs., *tóṅ-ḱor-la bor* (*thong khor la bor*) W. do not take them (the horses) farther than you can see them; the horizon Cs.; mtoṅ-dúg (mthong dug) ('eye-poison') evil-eye Sch.; envy, grudge, jealousy. — mtoṅ-snáṅ (mthong snang) v. snáṅ-ba (snang ba). — mtoṅ-byéd (mthong byed) that which sees, the eye Cs.; the substance which is the source of vision, a species of gall, चालोचक (cālocaka) Med. — mtoṅ-lám (mthong lam) the path of obtaining the power of sight, a mystical state Was. (139) — mtóṅ-lugs (mthong lugs) the way of beholding, of viewing a thing; notion, idea, opinion = snáṅ-ba (snang ba), mtóṅ-lugs ɣsúm-du byuṅ (mthong lugs gsum du byung) three different opinions were forming Glr.

1. an opening for the smoke in a ceiling or roof, also mtóṅs-kuṅ (mthongs khung). — 2. also mtóṅs-ka (mthongs ka), pavilion, platform, open gallery, on a flat roof Glr. (Cs.: 'impluvium, or the opening in the middle of a square building', for which, however, the Tibetan word seems to be kyams (khyams) or kyams-mtóṅs (khyams mthongs)).
silk ornaments on the borders of a painting Cs.
Cs.: to lose one's senses; perh. _tóms-pa ('thoms pa).
Lex.; Cs. 1. azure, sky-blue (?). — 2. n. of a flower. — 3. Glr. one of the five celestial gems; mtón-ka čén-po (mthon ka chen po) another of these gems. —
high, elevated, B. and col. (cf. mtó-ba (mtho ba)), of water deep, of the voice loud, of weight and measure full, of rank high; *čós-skad tón-po* (*chos skad thon po*) W. high-
